title: Workshop / pipeline refinements — evidence-grounded roadmap
question: Given the evidence from the workshop runs to date (the first complete draft, the FGAP-018/019/022 fixes, the WF-13/15/16 failures), what refinements are warranted — independent of any single run?
method: synthesis of the evaluated draft (run ...092307) + the fix/finding record; not a code probe.
findings_summary: A prioritized menu of refinement OPPORTUNITIES (not committed work; each becomes a task/FGAP when prioritized). Full body: docs/workshop-refinements-2026-06-07.md. (A) Draft content: A1 action-scope concentration -- the produced plan was governance-centric (6 steps = one policy-drafting cycle, 5/6 Principal's-Office-owned); prompt decompose-action-steps for a MIX of governance + distributed instructional actions (clearest content weakness). A2 criteria measurability -- 5/6 criteria were judgment; steer toward inspection/target with metrics. A3 soft-flags as a standing tuning signal -- recurring flags (e.g. 'HOD' abbreviation) -> prompt-reinforcement loop (DEC-40: prompt-side, never gate-loosening). (B) Pipeline: B4 generalize the grounding-leanness audit -- FGAP-022 scoped the atom inventory (sent to 13/14 specs that don't use it); audit ALL grounding sections + the whole-draft_state threading per spec (candidates: accreditation_standards, policies, prior_plans). B5 per-step parallel decomposition (R-0002) -- fan out the 5 step-iterating specs per action-step: speed + lighter calls (cap relief) + the production-trio realization. B6 upstream stdout-cap durability (WF-16) -- raise/remove the 10MB cap / quieter pi mode / disk-read independent of the tail. (C) Evaluation: C7 automated draft quality rubric -- score the DISC-26 11 axes + the FGAP-009 gate for a deterministic promotion verdict + regression catch. LEVERAGE RANK: 1) B4 grounding-leanness (cheap, broad, compounds), 2) A1 action-scope concreteness, 3) B5 per-step decomposition; then A2, A3, B6, C7.
